name: grace_full_lifecycle_clean_expiry
description: Warm day. Sensor opens at 13:00 (pause). Sensor closes at 13:10 (grace starts, 5-min grace). Sensor stays closed. Grace expires at 13:15 with all sensors closed → automation resumes cleanly. Classification cycle at 13:30 applies normally.
notes: Tests the normal (clean) grace expiry path: pause → sensor closes → grace expires cleanly → classification resumes.
Contrast with grace_full_lifecycle_sensor_still_open.json which tests the re-pause path.
Issue #476: migrated to use_coordinator=true. sensor_open/sensor_close now dispatch via
fake_hass.states.async_set() on the real door_window_sensors entity, reaching the coordinator's
actual _async_door_window_changed listener — real debounce (0s in the harness default),
real pause/resume, and a real async_call_later grace timer (manual_grace_seconds=300, fired via
scheduler.advance_to() at grace_expired). The synthetic grace_start/grace_end marker events are
removed — real grace now flows entirely from the sensor_close event's own dispatch chain.
skip_startup_coalesce=true required (see away_setpoint_change_not_override.json's notes for why).
Migrating this scenario surfaced two real harness bugs, both fixed as part of #476:
(1) dt_util.parse_datetime() was never patched by FakeScheduler.installed() — coordinator code
reading persisted ISO timestamps (including the stuck-grace detection at coordinator.py:1668,
6623-6624) got a MagicMock back, crashing on comparison. (2) A coordinator-mode state dispatch
(fake_hass.states.async_set) enqueues the real listener as an async task that does not run
synchronously — without a settle step after EVERY event dispatch (not just the last one), the
queued listener only ran when some UNRELATED heap entry happened to be due, at THAT entry's
time, not the actual dispatch time (confirmed via a ~5-minute timestamp drift caused by the
periodic 5-min thermal-sampler tick incidentally draining the queue). Both fixes live in
tools/sim_harness/ (fake_scheduler.py, run_production.py, build_coordinator.py), not production code.
Revert test: with config.door_window_sensors=[] (no listener registered for
binary_sensor.back_door), the sensor_open/sensor_close events still dispatch but no
_async_door_window_changed listener exists to react — event_log shows zero pause/resume/grace
events at all, correctly failing the paused (13:00) and resumed (13:10) assertions. Confirms this
scenario genuinely exercises the real coordinator listener, not a vacuous pass.

events.note: Hot afternoon. Outdoor too warm for nat vent.
Door opens. Nat vent ineligible (outdoor too warm). Automation pauses.
Door closes. All sensors closed → automation resumes, real grace timer starts (5 min).
30-min classification cycle. Grace is gone, no sensors open — classification applies normally.

assertions.reason: Sensor opens with outdoor (88F) > indoor (76F) — nat vent ineligible. Automation pauses.
All sensors closed — automation resumes. Grace period begins.

Grace expired cleanly (sensors stayed closed). Classification cycle at 13:30 applies normally (not blocked by a stuck pause) — real apply_classification() runs and re-arms the comfort band. Corrected from 'classification_applied' during #476 migration: real apply_classification() dedupes (automation.py:1355-1356) and only emits classification_applied when (day_type, hvac_mode) differs from the last applied classification — since this scenario dispatches the SAME classification (warm/cool) both at 12:31 and 13:30, the second call correctly suppresses the redundant event while still re-arming the band (comfort_band_applied does fire). The legacy simulator apparently did not model this dedup. comfort_band_armed is the correct assertion for 'classification cycle ran, unblocked' without requiring a specific (possibly deduped) event.

verdict.summary: Grace period expires cleanly when sensors stay closed; classification resumes normally
verdict.observed_behavior: paused (13:00) → resumed (13:10) → grace expires cleanly (13:15) → comfort_band_armed (13:30, classification cycle unblocked)
verdict.expected_behavior: When no sensor opens during the grace window, automation resumes without re-pause after grace expires
